members.name: GetAttributeCount
members.summary: Returns the number of attributes in the currently parsed element. [b]Note:[/b] If this method is used while the currently parsed node is not [constant NODE_ELEMENT] or [constant NODE_ELEMENT_END], this count will not be updated and will still reflect the last element.

members.name: GetAttributeName
members.summary: Returns the name of an attribute of the currently parsed element, specified by the [param idx] index.

members.name: GetAttributeValue
members.summary: Returns the value of an attribute of the currently parsed element, specified by the [param idx] index.

members.name: GetCurrentLine
members.summary: Returns the current line in the parsed file, counting from 0.

members.name: GetNamedAttributeValue
members.summary: Returns the value of an attribute of the currently parsed element, specified by its [param name]. This method will raise an error if the element has no such attribute.

members.name: GetNamedAttributeValueSafe
members.summary: Returns the value of an attribute of the currently parsed element, specified by its [param name]. This method will return an empty string if the element has no such attribute.

members.name: GetNodeData
members.summary: Returns the contents of a text node. This method will raise an error if the current parsed node is of any other type.

members.name: GetNodeName
members.summary: Returns the name of a node. This method will raise an error if the currently parsed node is a text node. [b]Note:[/b] The content of a [constant NODE_CDATA] node and the comment string of a [constant NODE_COMMENT] node are also considered names.

members.name: GetNodeOffset
members.summary: Returns the byte offset of the currently parsed node since the beginning of the file or buffer. This is usually equivalent to the number of characters before the read position.

members.name: GetNodeType
members.summary: Returns the type of the current node. Compare with [enum NodeType] constants.

members.name: HasAttribute
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the currently parsed element has an attribute with the [param name].

members.name: IsEmpty
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the currently parsed element is empty, e.g. [code]<element />[/code].

members.name: Open
members.summary: Opens an XML [param file] for parsing. This method returns an error code.

members.name: OpenBuffer
members.summary: Opens an XML raw [param buffer] for parsing. This method returns an error code.

members.name: Read
members.summary: Parses the next node in the file. This method returns an error code.

members.name: Seek
members.summary: Moves the buffer cursor to a certain offset (since the beginning) and reads the next node there. This method returns an error code.

members.name: SkipSection
members.summary: Skips the current section. If the currently parsed node contains more inner nodes, they will be ignored and the cursor will go to the closing of the current element.

values.name: NODE_CDATA
values.summary: A node type for CDATA (Character Data) sections, e.g. [code]<![CDATA[CDATA section]]>[/code].

values.name: NODE_COMMENT
values.summary: A comment node type, e.g. [code]<!--A comment-->[/code].

values.name: NODE_ELEMENT
values.summary: An element node type, also known as a tag, e.g. [code]<title>[/code].

values.name: NODE_ELEMENT_END
values.summary: An end of element node type, e.g. [code]</title>[/code].

values.name: NODE_NONE
values.summary: There's no node (no file or buffer opened).

values.name: NODE_TEXT
values.summary: A text node type, i.e. text that is not inside an element. This includes whitespace.

constants.name: NODE_NONE
constants.summary: There's no node (no file or buffer opened).

constants.name: NODE_ELEMENT
constants.summary: An element node type, also known as a tag, e.g. [code]<title>[/code].

constants.name: NODE_ELEMENT_END
constants.summary: An end of element node type, e.g. [code]</title>[/code].

constants.name: NODE_TEXT
constants.summary: A text node type, i.e. text that is not inside an element. This includes whitespace.

constants.name: NODE_COMMENT
constants.summary: A comment node type, e.g. [code]<!--A comment-->[/code].

constants.name: NODE_CDATA
constants.summary: A node type for CDATA (Character Data) sections, e.g. [code]<![CDATA[CDATA section]]>[/code].
